test.decisive_call: MONITOR · draining liquidity (composite 18.1/100) + widening credit + easy bank standards — trim small caps, EM, and HY by 30%, raise cash to 15%, increase gold and duration as the canary signals (CCC HY OAS, TGA, primary credit) are already at watch level.

liquidity.narrative: Liquidity is gradually tightening — early-cycle stress. TGA $878B above $800B watch — Treasury issuance draining liquidity · RRP $0B near-zero — MMF backstop nearly exhausted · Primary credit $3.48B — banks accessing discount window
drivers: TGA $878B above $800B watch — Treasury issuance draining liquidity
RRP $0B near-zero — MMF backstop nearly exhausted
Primary credit $3.48B — banks accessing discount window

credit.narrative: Credit spreads widening at the bottom of the quality spectrum first — early-cycle differentiation. CCC HY OAS 9.15% (canary stressed; GFC peak 22%, COVID 19%)
drivers: CCC HY OAS 9.15% (canary stressed; GFC peak 22%, COVID 19%)

lending.narrative: Banks at or below midpoint on standards across categories. Credit channel functional; loan demand mixed but not collapsing.

us_large_equity.rationale: Watch state — maintain core but tilt cautious

us_small_cap.rationale: Bank-tightening cycle starting hits IWM first

dollar.rationale: Defensive USD bid in tightening cycle

emerging_markets.rationale: Tightening USD liquidity weighs on EM

target_allocation.rationale: Underweight — small caps vulnerable to bank tightening
Underweight — credit spreads at watch level
Add long duration as defensive bid

key_risks: CCC HY OAS crossing 12% (escalation to ELEVATED)
CB swap lines drawn (FX dollar shortage)
Next SLOOS showing >25% net tightening on C&I or CRE
